current_turn_information: I have not had any fever or night sweats.
I did develop a fever in the hospital a few days ago, but it was after I started getting intravenous fluids and before I was admitted — it went up to 100.9°F.
I don’t think it’s recent or ongoing now.
I also don’t have night sweats.
My fatigue and cough are still present, but I don’t have weight loss or any other symptoms like that.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old male with MDS, trisomy 8, history of azacitadine and lenalidomide therapy, currently off therapy and transfusions for anemia and thrombocytopenia. Presented with fatigue and cough. Recent history includes pseudomonas otitis externa, hospitalization for vertigo, prednisone and antibiotics completed a week ago. Physical exam shows mild bibasilar crackles. Labs show Hgb 5.8. Final result: viral pneumonia, unspecified. Microbiology tests were inconclusive due to inadequate specimen.

current_turn_information: I have not had any fever or night sweats. I did develop a fever in the hospital a few days ago, but it was after I started getting intravenous fluids and before I was admitted — it went up to 100.9°F. I don’t think it’s recent or ongoing now. I also don’t have night sweats. My fatigue and cough are still present, but I don’t have weight loss or any other symptoms like that.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old M with MDS, trisomy 8, progressed despite azacitadine and lenalidomide, now off therapy and transfusions for anemia, chronic thrombocytopenia, no major bleeding history, chronic leukocytosis. Presents with fever and fatigue. Recent history: pseudomonas otitis externa on cipro/augmentin, hospitalized for vertigo, discharged after prednisone and antibiotic course (finished ~1 week ago). Last dose of prednisone was ___; last ___ (6 days ago) developed profound generalized fatigue. No localizing symptoms except diffuse muscle cramps (long-standing). Denies ear pain, fever at home, mucous worsening, nocturia (resolved), dysuria, rash, headaches, visual changes, bloody stools, diarrhea, vomiting. Last BM formed this morning. Clinic reported fatigue. Hgb 5.8, Hct improvement (bibasilar opacities resolved, persistent lingular opacity). Hypovolemic due to decreased PO intake, received 1L saline, temp rose to 99.4, 99.6, then 100.9. Admitted after discussion with Dr. ___; labs also noted. Follow-up for MDS, refractory anemia with ringed sideroblasts (WHO classification), initially low-risk (IPSS). On arrival, appears well, denies complaints other than cough. Physical exam: alert, tired, T 100.2, BP 126/60, HR 90, RR 20, 99% RA. Skin dry, HEENT pale pink, lungs clear, cardiac normal, abdomen soft, extremities symmetrical. Discharge: Tm 101.6, BP 110/56, HR 70, RR 22, SpO2 94%, lungs mild bibasilar crackles. Right-sided port. Microbiology: respiratory viral culture cancelled (inadequate specimen), viral antigen screen — no result. Final result: viral pneumonia, unspecified.

current_turn_information: I used to smoke, but I’ve quit for several years now.
I’ve also been exposed to some environmental irritants over the years—mainly from working in construction, where there was a lot of dust and fumes.
I’ve had a few respiratory issues since then, but I’ve tried to avoid those environments as much as possible.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 67-year-old male with MDS, trisomy 8, on prior azacitadine and lenalidomide, now off therapy and transfusions for anemia and thrombocytopenia. Presented with fatigue and cough. History of pseudomonas otitis externa, recent hospitalization for vertigo, completed prednisone and antibiotics. Physical exam: alert, tired, mild bibasilar crackles. Labs: Hgb 5.8. Final result: viral pneumonia, unspecified. No fever or night sweats currently. No weight loss or other systemic symptoms. No respiratory history of smoking or occupational exposure mentioned previously.

current_turn_information: I have not had any fever or night sweats. I did develop a fever in the hospital a few days ago, but it was after I started getting intravenous fluids and before I was admitted — it went up to 100.9°F. I don’t think it’s recent or ongoing now. I also don’t have night sweats. My fatigue and cough are still present, but I don’t have weight loss or any other symptoms like that.
As far as I know, I haven't had any problems in that area, and I haven't been told of any abnormal findings related to it.

I used to smoke, but I’ve quit for several years now. I’ve also been exposed to some environmental irritants over the years—mainly from working in construction, where there was a lot of dust and fumes. I’ve had a few respiratory issues since then, but I’ve tried to avoid those environments as much as possible.

positive_experience.situation: When a patient presents with redness, swelling, and pus around a nail, and the history suggests recent trauma or nail-biting, but no imaging or lab results are available, what should the doctor do next?
positive_experience.action: Update the differential to include paronychia and confirm the diagnosis with targeted patient history about trauma or nail manipulation, then finalize only after ruling out onychia or other nail infections.
positive_experience.boundary: Use this memory when symptoms suggest paronychia and no imaging or labs are available; do not use if there is evidence of nail bed involvement or if the patient has systemic signs of infection.

positive_experience.situation: When a patient presents with dull, aching hip pain worsening over months with stiffness and swelling, especially after trauma, and no imaging or lab results are available, what should the doctor do next?
positive_experience.action: Request targeted imaging of the hip to rule out structural damage or other causes, then update the differential based on findings.
positive_experience.boundary: Use when hip pain is chronic, mechanical, and associated with trauma or stiffness; do not use if imaging is already available or if systemic symptoms suggest infection or malignancy.

positive_experience.situation: When a patient presents with localized aching hip pain that worsens with movement and is associated with morning stiffness and functional limitation, but no prior history or lab results are available, what should the doctor do next?
positive_experience.action: Request a focused physical exam to assess joint mobility, crepitus, and gait, then review imaging if available to confirm joint space narrowing or osteophytes.
positive_experience.boundary: Use when pain is localized, mechanical, and associated with stiffness; do not use if there is systemic inflammation, trauma history, or imaging showing fracture or infection.
